figure.title: Dual Operand 8-Byte Atomic Operation Data Packing
figure.purpose: Shows the layout for a dual-operand (Op1/Op2) 8-byte Atomic operation and its returned data.

technical_summary: Operands are 8 bytes each, spanning two Doublewords.
Byte Enables are 0xFF for each active 8-byte operand element.
Op1 Element0 is at DW1:DW0, Op1 Element1 is at DW5:DW4.
Op2 Element0 is at DW9:DW8, Op2 Element1 is at DW13:DW12.
Returned Data for AtomicR is returned in the locations corresponding to Op1 (DW1:DW0, DW5:DW4).

packet_layout.description: Originator Data channel carrying 8-byte Op1 and Op2 operands
Read Response Data channel carrying 8-byte returned values in Op1 locations

critical_facts: For dual-operand Atomics, Op1 and Op2 elements are interleaved or placed in specific DW pairs, and return data replaces the Op1 positions.
